items.briefing:
Good news for Australian organisations: recent data shows phishing risk drops significantly after staff complete security awareness training, a strong reminder that education works. However, threats are evolving fast — ESET is warning of a surge in AI-assisted attacks and "quishing" (QR code phishing), while a separate report shows a lone attacker used AI tools to compromise an AWS cloud environment in just 72 hours.

Staff should be especially cautious of unexpected QR codes in emails or printed materials, as these are increasingly used to bypass traditional security filters. **Practical tip:** Never scan a QR code from an unsolicited email or document without verifying the source first.

featured.title: Australia & New Zealand phishing risk drops after training
featured.quote: Australian workforce phishing vulnerability dropped to 5.3% after security awareness training.
